name: Common Cold
description: A viral upper respiratory infection causing sneezing, sore throat and runny nose.
transmission: Respiratory droplets from coughing or sneezing, and direct contact with contaminated surfaces.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished from flu by absence of high fever and severe body aches; from allergies by presence of sore throat and thicker mucus.
prevention_tips: Avoid close contact with infected individuals

name: Influenza
description: Influenza (flu) causes fever, body aches and respiratory symptoms. Can be severe for high-risk groups.
transmission: Highly contagious; spread through respiratory droplets and contact with contaminated surfaces.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished from common cold by sudden onset of high fever, severe fatigue, and prominent muscle/body aches.
measures: seek antiviral within 48 hours if high risk

name: Gastroenteritis
description: Inflammation of stomach and intestines causing vomiting and diarrhea.
transmission: Fecal-oral route through contaminated food, water, or contact with infected individuals.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished from food poisoning by longer incubation and duration; from cholera by less severe 'rice-water' diarrhea.
prevention_tips: Thorough hand washing after using toilet
measures: seek care if blood in stool or high fever

name: Malaria
description: A mosquito-borne parasitic infection causing cycles of fever, chills and flu-like symptoms. Severe cases can affect the brain, kidneys or cause anemia.
transmission: Spread by the bite of an infected female Anopheles mosquito.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished by cyclical fever patterns (paroxysms); travels history to endemic areas is a key indicator.
measures: seek medical care for diagnostic blood test
seek immediate help if confusion or severe weakness appears

name: Dengue
description: A mosquito-borne viral infection causing high fever, severe joint pain, and rash. Severe dengue can lead to hemorrhage or shock.
transmission: Spread by the bite of infected Aedes aegypti and Aedes albopictus mosquitoes.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished from Malaria by presence of rash and 'breakbone' joint pain; bleeding gums/warning signs indicate severity.
prevention_tips: Eliminate standing water where mosquitoes breed
measures: watch for warning signs: abdominal pain, bleeding, vomiting
seek hospital care if symptoms worsen

name: Typhoid
description: A bacterial infection caused by Salmonella Typhi, leading to prolonged fever, abdominal pain and digestive issues.
transmission: Fecal-oral route through contaminated food or water.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished by 'rose spots' on trunk (sometimes) and slower heart rate despite high fever; gastrointestinal symptoms are more prolonged than simple gastroenteritis.
measures: start antibiotics prescribed by a doctor
seek medical help if persistent vomiting or bleeding

name: Chikungunya
description: A mosquito-borne viral disease marked by sudden high fever and severe joint pain that can last weeks.
transmission: Spread by the bite of infected Aedes mosquitoes.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished from Dengue by more prominent and persistent joint pain/stiffness, often affecting small joints of hands and feet.
measures: avoid NSAIDs until dengue is ruled out

name: Japanese Encephalitis
description: A viral brain infection spread by mosquitoes, leading to swelling of the brain. Can cause seizures and neurological damage.
transmission: Spread by Culex mosquitoes, primarily in rural areas near rice paddies and pig farms.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished from common meningitis by presence of movement disorders and rapid progression to severe neurological symptoms; specific rural exposures are key.
prevention_tips: JE vaccination for residents and travelers
measures: family should watch for confusion or loss of consciousness

name: Tuberculosis
description: A bacterial lung infection caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, leading to chronic cough and weight loss. Can spread through air.
transmission: Airborne; spread when an infected person coughs, sneezes, or speaks.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished from common pneumonia by chronicity (symptoms lasting >2-3 weeks), night sweats, and significant weight loss.
prevention_tips: Early treatment of latent TB infection
measures: start full course of anti-TB medicines

name: Cholera
description: A water-borne bacterial disease causing severe watery diarrhea and dehydration. Can cause death within hours.
transmission: Ingestion of contaminated food or water, especially water contaminated with the feces of an infected person.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished by the sudden onset of massive 'rice-water' stools and rapid dehydration; much more severe than typical gastroenteritis.
prevention_tips: Drink only boiled, chlorinated, or bottled water

name: Leptospirosis
description: A bacterial infection spread through water contaminated with animal urine. Can affect kidneys or liver.
transmission: Contact with water, soil, or food contaminated with urine from infected animals (e.g., rats).
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished by exposure history (flooding, water sports) and often presents with calf muscle pain and conjunctival suffusion (redness of eyes without discharge).
prevention_tips: Avoid swimming or wading in potentially contaminated water
Wear protective clothing/footwear in wet areas
measures: seek care if yellowing of eyes or decreased urine

name: Hepatitis A
description: A viral liver infection spread through contaminated food or water, causing jaundice and digestive symptoms.
transmission: Fecal-oral route through contaminated food or water.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished from other hepatitis viruses by acute onset and often associated with food/water outbreaks; rarely becomes chronic compared to Hep B/C.
prevention_tips: Avoid tap water and raw foods in areas with poor sanitation
measures: seek care for persistent vomiting or confusion

name: Hepatitis B
description: A viral liver infection that can become chronic and cause serious liver damage including cirrhosis and cancer.
transmission: Contact with infectious blood, semen, and other body fluids; mother-to-child at birth.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished by risk factors (needles, unprotected contact) and specific blood tests (HBsAg); often more likely to lead to chronic disease than Hep A.
prevention_tips: Avoid sharing personal items like razors/toothbrushes
Use sterile equipment for any medical or cosmetic procedures

name: Hepatitis C
description: A viral liver infection that often becomes chronic and may lead to cirrhosis or liver cancer if untreated.
transmission: Primarily through blood-to-blood contact (e.g., sharing needles, unsterile medical procedures).
differential_diagnosis: Often asymptomatic in early stages; distinguished from Hep A/B by blood test (anti-HCV) and history of blood exposure.
prevention_tips: Ensure use of sterile injection/medical equipment

name: Hepatitis E
description: A viral liver infection spread by contaminated water. Usually resolves on its own but can be severe in pregnant women.
transmission: Fecal-oral route, primarily through contaminated drinking water.
differential_diagnosis: High clinical suspicion in pregnant women with jaundice; outbreaks are often associated with poor water infrastructure.
vaccines: Hepatitis E vaccine (available in some countries)

name: Measles
description: A highly contagious viral infection causing fever, cough, and a characteristic rash. Can lead to pneumonia or encephalitis.

name: Mumps
description: A viral infection affecting the salivary glands, causing swelling near the jaw and fever.
measures: seek care if severe abdominal pain (possible pancreatitis)

name: Rubella
description: A viral infection causing fever and rash. Dangerous in pregnancy as it can harm the fetus.

name: Pneumonia
description: An infection causing inflammation of the lungs. Can be viral, bacterial or fungal. Severity ranges from mild to life-threatening.

name: Polio
description: A viral infection that can cause paralysis. Most cases are asymptomatic but severe cases can be life-threatening.
measures: seek urgent care if weakness or paralysis occurs

name: Rabies
description: A viral infection that affects the nervous system. Once symptoms appear, rabies is almost always fatal.

name: Tetanus
description: A bacterial toxin-mediated disease causing severe muscle spasms. Can be fatal without treatment.

name: Diphtheria
description: A bacterial infection affecting the throat. Can block breathing and release dangerous toxins.

name: Leprosy
description: A chronic infectious disease caused by Mycobacterium leprae affecting skin and peripheral nerves, leading to numbness and skin lesions.

name: COVID-19
description: An infectious respiratory disease caused by SARS-CoV-2, ranging from mild respiratory illness to severe pneumonia and systemic complications.
transmission: Respiratory droplets/aerosols from an infected person's breath, cough, or sneeze.
differential_diagnosis: Distinguished by loss of taste/smell (highly specific) and often more severe/prolonged respiratory symptoms than the common cold.
prevention_tips: Wear masks in crowded indoor settings
measures: supportive care at home for mild cases
seek urgent care for breathing difficulty

name: Influenza
description: Influenza (flu) is a viral respiratory infection causing fever, cough, and body aches; can be severe in high-risk groups.
measures: antiviral therapy for high-risk or early cases

name: Pneumonia
description: An infection causing inflammation of the lung air sacs; can be bacterial, viral, or fungal and range from mild to life-threatening.
measures: oxygen and supportive care for severe cases

name: Common Cold
description: A viral upper respiratory infection typically caused by rhinoviruses producing nasal symptoms, sore throat and cough.

name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
description: A metabolic disorder characterized by insulin resistance and high blood glucose; long-term complications include cardiovascular disease, kidney disease, and neuropathy.
measures: oral hypoglycemics or insulin as prescribed

name: Hypertension (High Blood Pressure)
description: A chronic condition where arterial blood pressure is persistently elevated, increasing risk of heart disease and stroke.
measures: lifestyle modification (salt reduction, exercise, weight loss)

name: Heart Disease (Ischemic Heart Disease)
description: A group of conditions, including coronary artery disease, where reduced blood flow to the heart causes chest pain and increases risk of heart attack.
measures: seek urgent care for chest pain

name: Stroke (Cerebrovascular Accident)
description: An acute neurological event due to interrupted blood flow to the brain (ischemic or hemorrhagic), requiring immediate emergency care.
measures: thrombolysis or thrombectomy if ischemic and within window

name: Breast Cancer
description: A malignant tumor of breast tissue that may present as a lump or skin/nipple changes; early detection improves outcomes.
measures: surgical and oncologic treatment as advised

name: Appendicitis
description: Inflammation of the appendix causing severe abdominal pain, often requiring surgical removal (appendicectomy).

name: Gallstones
description: Solid particles (stones) that form in the gallbladder; may be asymptomatic or cause biliary colic and require cholecystectomy.

name: Hepatitis D
description: A liver infection (HDV) that only occurs with hepatitis B virus; causes more severe disease and faster progression to cirrhosis.
vaccines: Hepatitis B vaccine (prevents Hepatitis D by preventing HBV infection)

name: Hepatitis G
description: Also known as GBV-C; a virus detected in humans but not clearly linked to liver disease; clinical significance is limited.

name: Yellow Fever
description: A mosquito-borne viral infection that can cause fever, jaundice and severe hemorrhagic illness; preventable with a highly effective vaccine.
vaccines: Yellow fever vaccine (single dose confers long-lasting protection)

name: Plague
description: A bacterial infection (Yersinia pestis) transmitted by fleas or droplets; can present as bubonic, septicemic or pneumonic plague and can be life-threatening.

name: Smallpox
description: A historically devastating viral disease caused by variola virus; declared eradicated in 1980; vaccines exist but routine vaccination stopped.
vaccines: Smallpox vaccine (used historically; stockpiles maintained for emergency use)

name: Anthrax
description: Infection caused by Bacillus anthracis; can present as cutaneous, inhalational or gastrointestinal anthrax; serious but preventable in at-risk groups with vaccine.
vaccines: Anthrax vaccine (recommended for certain high-risk occupational groups)

name: Leishmaniasis
description: Parasitic disease transmitted by sandflies causing cutaneous, mucocutaneous or visceral forms; treatment and vector control are mainstays; no widely used human vaccine yet.

name: Filariasis
description: Parasitic infection (e.g., Wuchereria bancrofti) causing lymphatic damage, lymphedema and elephantiasis; prevented by mass drug administration rather than a vaccine.

name: Japanese B Encephalitis
description: A mosquito-borne viral encephalitis caused by Japanese encephalitis virus (JEV); can cause severe neurologic disease; effective vaccines are available.

name: Rabies (Animal Bite)
description: A viral infection transmitted by animal bites that is almost always fatal once symptoms appear; preventable with prompt post-exposure prophylaxis (vaccine + immunoglobulin).

name: Scabies
description: Skin infestation by the Sarcoptes scabiei mite causing intense itching and a rash; treated with topical scabicides or oral medication.

name: Tinea (Fungal Infections)
description: Superficial fungal infections of skin, hair or nails (tinea) such as ringworm; treated with topical or oral antifungals.

name: Psoriasis
description: A chronic immune-mediated skin condition causing red, scaly plaques; treatments control symptoms but there is no cure.

name: Vitiligo
description: A condition where patches of skin lose pigment (depigmentation) due to melanocyte loss; not contagious and treatment focuses on cosmetic management or repigmentation.

name: Eczema
description: Also called atopic dermatitis; a chronic itchy inflammatory skin condition often starting in childhood and managed with emollients and topical steroids.

name: Obstructive Sleep Apnea
description: A sleep disorder where airway collapse causes repeated pauses in breathing during sleep; treated with lifestyle measures, CPAP, devices or surgery.

name: GERD (Acid Reflux)
description: Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) causes recurrent heartburn due to stomach acid flowing back into the oesophagus; managed with lifestyle changes and acid suppression.

name: Cystic Fibrosis
description: An inherited genetic disorder (CFTR gene) causing thick mucus in lungs and digestive issues; chronic condition managed with physiotherapy, antibiotics and CFTR modulators.

name: Asthma
description: A chronic inflammatory airway condition that causes recurrent episodes of wheezing, coughing and breathing difficulty.

name: Bronchitis
description: Inflammation of the bronchial tubes often causing persistent cough and mucus production.

name: Obesity
description: A chronic health condition involving excessive body fat that increases risk of metabolic and cardiovascular diseases.

name: HIV/AIDS
description: A viral infection that weakens the immune system, increasing susceptibility to opportunistic infections.

name: Ebola
description: A severe viral hemorrhagic fever transmitted through body fluids, often life-threatening.

name: SARS
description: A viral respiratory illness caused by SARS-CoV leading to pneumonia-like symptoms.

name: Zika
description: A mosquito-borne viral infection that can cause fever and rash; can lead to birth defects if contracted during pregnancy.

name: Migraine
description: A neurological condition causing intense headaches often accompanied by nausea and sensitivity to light and sound.

name: Parkinson's
description: A progressive neurological disorder affecting movement due to dopamine loss in the brain.

name: Alzheimer's
description: A degenerative brain disease leading to progressive memory loss and cognitive decline.

name: Epilepsy
description: A neurological condition characterized by recurrent seizures caused by abnormal electrical activity in the brain.

name: Chronic Kidney Disease
description: A long-term condition where kidneys gradually lose function, leading to fluid imbalance and toxin buildup.

name: Acute Kidney Injury
description: A sudden decline in kidney function caused by illness, dehydration or injury.

name: Urinary Tract Infection
description: A bacterial infection affecting the urinary tract, most commonly the bladder.

name: Gastroenteritis
description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines often caused by viral or bacterial infection.

name: Peptic Ulcer
description: Sores that develop in the lining of the stomach or duodenum often due to H. pylori infection or NSAID use.

name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome
description: A functional bowel disorder causing abdominal discomfort and changes in bowel habits.

name: Arthritis
description: Inflammation of one or more joints causing pain and stiffness that typically worsens with age.

name: Asthma
description: A chronic respiratory condition causing inflammation and narrowing of airways, leading to breathing difficulties.
measures: Seek emergency care for severe attacks

name: Hypertension
description: High blood pressure that can lead to serious health problems like heart disease and stroke if left untreated.

name: Migraine
description: A neurological condition causing intense, debilitating headaches often accompanied by nausea and sensitivity to light.

name: Jaundice
description: Yellowing of skin and eyes due to high bilirubin, often from liver problems.

name: Heat Stroke
description: Serious heat illness common in hot summers.

name: Dehydration
description: Loss of body fluids, common in hot weather.

name: Food Poisoning
description: Illness from contaminated food.

name: Fungal Skin Infection
description: Fungal infections common in humid conditions.

name: Conjunctivitis
description: Eye inflammation, very contagious.

name: Asthma
description: A chronic respiratory condition causing inflammation and narrowing of airways, leading to breathing difficulty and wheezing.
measures: take controller medications daily if prescribed
seek emergency care for severe attacks

name: Hypertension
description: High blood pressure that can lead to heart disease, stroke, and kidney problems if left untreated.

name: Migraine
description: A neurological condition causing intense, throbbing headaches often on one side of the head, lasting hours to days.
measures: identify and avoid triggers (stress, certain foods)

name: Allergic Rhinitis
description: An allergic reaction causing inflammation of the nasal passages due to allergens like pollen, dust, or pet dander.
measures: keep windows closed during high pollen days
wash bedding regularly in hot water

name: GERD (Acid Reflux)
description: Gastroesophageal reflux disease where stomach acid flows back into the esophagus, causing irritation and discomfort.
measures: don't lie down immediately after eating

name: Anxiety Disorder
description: A mental health condition characterized by persistent, excessive worry and fear that interferes with daily activities.

name: Insomnia
description: A sleep disorder characterized by difficulty falling asleep or staying asleep, leading to poor quality sleep.
measures: exercise regularly but not before bed
keep bedroom cool, dark, and quiet

name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
description: A bacterial infection affecting any part of the urinary system, most commonly the bladder and urethra.
measures: cranberry juice may help prevent recurrence
seek care if fever or back pain develops

name: Iron Deficiency Anemia
description: A condition where blood lacks adequate healthy red blood cells due to insufficient iron, leading to reduced oxygen delivery to tissues.
measures: eat iron-rich foods (spinach, red meat, beans)
consume vitamin C to enhance iron absorption
get blood tests to monitor levels

name: Osteoarthritis
description: A degenerative joint disease where cartilage breaks down, causing pain and stiffness, commonly affecting knees, hips, and hands.
measures: consider joint injections for severe cases

name: Eczema (Atopic Dermatitis)
description: A chronic skin condition causing inflammation, itching, and red, dry patches, often triggered by allergens or irritants.
measures: keep nails short to prevent scratching damage

name: Constipation
description: A digestive condition characterized by difficulty passing stools or infrequent bowel movements.
measures: increase fiber intake (fruits, vegetables, whole grains)
drink plenty of water (8+ glasses daily)

name: Sinusitis
description: Inflammation of the sinuses causing congestion, facial pressure, and thick nasal discharge, often following a cold.

name: Acne
description: A skin condition occurring when hair follicles become clogged with oil and dead skin cells, causing pimples and blemishes.
measures: wash face twice daily with gentle cleanser
use benzoyl peroxide or salicylic acid products
maintain healthy diet, limit dairy and sugar

name: Vitamin D Deficiency
description: Insufficient vitamin D levels leading to weakened bones, fatigue, and increased infection risk.
measures: eat vitamin D-rich foods (fatty fish, egg yolks, fortified milk)
